qa_evidence.build: Generic iOS Simulator test build, admin TypeScript check and admin production build succeeded.
qa_evidence.static: Full scripts/run-static-qa.sh suite and DATA PROJECT validation succeeded with 102 governed records.
qa_evidence.duplicate: No duplicate IDs, normalized titles, canonical websites or media IDs detected across nine batches.
qa_evidence.source: All twelve records use exact official primary-source pages; the full network audit checked 937 URLs and reported zero confirmed broken URLs.
qa_evidence.media: Government records contain no optional media assets; there are no unverified or unlicensed assets.
qa_evidence.search: Every record has at least three unique search terms and all related entity IDs resolve across the nine batches.
qa_evidence.ai: Every AI summary passes minimum length and placeholder-content validation.

records.title: Apply for a highly skilled migrant residence permit
records.description: A non-EU employee coming to the Netherlands as a highly skilled migrant needs a qualifying residence permit. Only an employer recognised by the IND can submit the application, and employment and income conditions apply.
records.ai_summary: Confirm that the employer is an IND-recognised sponsor and that the employment and current income conditions are met. Provide the requested legalised or translated documents to the employer and track the application and biometrics steps.

records.title: Arrange a residence permit for study
records.description: Students from outside the EU, EEA or Switzerland generally need a study residence permit for qualifying education in the Netherlands. The recognised educational institution normally acts as sponsor and submits the application to the IND.
records.ai_summary: Select the study-permit route matching the programme and confirm that the institution is an IND-recognised sponsor. Supply admission, identity, financial and any legalised documents requested by the institution and complete biometrics after filing.

records.title: Renounce another nationality after naturalisation
records.description: Some adults who become Dutch through naturalisation must give up their previous nationality, while statutory exceptions apply. The procedure is handled with the previous country's authorities and evidence must be provided to the IND when required.
records.ai_summary: Before naturalisation, check whether renunciation is legally required or an exception applies and understand the consequences. Follow the issuing country's procedure, keep the IND reachable and submit proof of the request and completed renunciation by the stated deadlines.

records.title: Register for the civic integration exam
records.description: People required or choosing to integrate can register for applicable language and knowledge exams through Mijn Inburgering. The required exams and level depend on the applicable integration act and the person's official integration plan.
records.ai_summary: Check Mijn Inburgering or the personal integration plan to identify the required exams and language level. Register through the portal with DigiD, choose an available date and location, and retain the confirmation and invitation.

records.title: Register with a family doctor
records.description: Registration with a general practitioner is not legally mandatory, but it is recommended so primary care is available when needed. A municipality or health insurer can help identify practices accepting patients in the area.

official_source.title: What do I need to arrange if I’m moving to the Netherlands?

records.ai_summary: Ask the health insurer or municipality which nearby general practices accept new patients. Contact the practice with identity, address and insurance details, and ask how urgent care and repeat prescriptions are handled before registration is complete.

records.title: Change Dutch health insurer
records.description: Policyholders can normally switch Dutch health insurers at the end of the calendar year. Cancellation and new-policy deadlines apply, while insurers must accept applicants for the statutory standard package regardless of health status.
records.ai_summary: Compare the coming year's policy conditions, contracted providers and total expected costs before switching. Cancel the old policy or use the new insurer's transfer service by the applicable deadline and ensure uninterrupted standard coverage.

attributes.responsible_authority: Health insurers under the Healthcare Insurance Act

records.title: Check a residential rent increase
records.description: Dutch rules limit or regulate annual rent increases depending on whether housing is social, midprice or private-sector. Tenants can check the notice, timing and permitted increase and may challenge qualifying disputes through the Rent Tribunal.

official_source.title: Welke regels gelden er voor een huurverhoging?

records.ai_summary: Identify the tenancy segment and compare the landlord's notice with the current legal cap, timing and contract. Preserve the notice and rent history, raise errors with the landlord and use the Rent Tribunal route when eligible.

attributes.responsible_authority: Ministry of Housing and Spatial Planning

records.title: Object to a WOZ property valuation
records.description: Owners or occupants who disagree with the WOZ value stated in a municipal decision can contact the municipality and submit a formal objection. The decision and municipal website explain the procedure and deadline.

official_source.title: Wat kan ik doen als ik het niet eens ben met de WOZ-waarde?

records.ai_summary: Review the valuation report and comparable properties, then contact the municipality to discuss evident errors. If unresolved, submit a reasoned objection through the method on the WOZ decision within six weeks of its date.

records.title: File a complaint about a public authority
records.description: A person can complain to a public authority about conduct such as unfair treatment or excessive delay when the issue is not instead subject to objection, appeal or court proceedings. Statutory admissibility conditions apply.

official_source.title: Filing a complaint about a public authority

records.ai_summary: First distinguish a conduct complaint from a formal objection or appeal against a decision. Submit the complaint to the authority concerned within the applicable period, describe the event and retain all correspondence and acknowledgements.

attributes.general_filing_period: Within one year of the event

records.title: Report discrimination
records.description: A person experiencing discrimination can seek advice or report it to the responsible organisation, a local anti-discrimination service, the national helpline, the Human Rights Institute, police or another competent body depending on the incident.
records.ai_summary: Record what happened, when, where and who was involved, and preserve messages or witness details. Choose the reporting body based on whether advice, organisational resolution, a human-rights assessment or criminal investigation is needed.

records.title: Apply for a Dutch consular certificate abroad
records.description: Dutch nationals living abroad may need a consular certificate for matters with Dutch or foreign authorities. Embassies and consulates can issue specified certificates, subject to identity, nationality and supporting-document requirements.
records.ai_summary: Ask the requesting organisation which certificate and issue date it accepts, then select the country-specific NetherlandsWorldwide instructions. Gather a valid Dutch passport or ID card plus the required address, civil-status and supporting evidence.

attributes.eligibility: Dutch national with valid passport or ID card

records.title: Obtain a certificate of life abroad
records.description: Pension and benefit organisations may require people living abroad to prove they are alive. Depending on the country and requesting organisation, proof may be submitted digitally or certified by an embassy, consulate, municipality or notary.
records.ai_summary: Confirm the requesting pension or benefit provider's accepted proof method and freshness requirement. Follow the country-specific instructions, bring valid identification and address evidence, and sign any form only in the authorised official's presence.

attributes.responsible_authority: Dutch embassy, consulate or accepted local authority
